sets.q: What is the hydrostatic pressure at a depth of 3 m in water? (ρ = 1 000 kg/m³, g = 10 m/s²)
Find the hydrostatic pressure at 5 m depth in water. (ρ = 1 000 kg/m³, g = 10 m/s²)
What is the hydrostatic pressure at 8 m depth in water? (ρ = 1 000 kg/m³, g = 10 m/s²)
What is the hydrostatic pressure at 12 m depth in water? (ρ = 1 000 kg/m³, g = 10 m/s²)
Calculate the hydrostatic pressure at 20 m depth in water. (ρ = 1 000 kg/m³, g = 10 m/s²)
What is the absolute pressure at 4 m depth in water? (ρ = 1 000 kg/m³, g = 10 m/s², atmospheric pressure = 101 325 Pa)
Find the absolute pressure at 10 m depth in water. (ρ = 1 000 kg/m³, g = 10 m/s², Patm = 101 325 Pa)
What is the absolute pressure at 2 m depth? (ρ = 1 000 kg/m³, g = 10 m/s², Patm = 101 325 Pa)
Calculate the absolute pressure at 6 m below the water surface. (ρ = 1 000 kg/m³, g = 10 m/s², Patm = 101 325 Pa)
Find the absolute pressure at 15 m depth. (ρ = 1 000 kg/m³, g = 10 m/s², Patm = 101 325 Pa)
What is the difference between gauge pressure and absolute pressure?
sets.given: Absolute pressure = gauge pressure + atmospheric pressure.
opts: Gauge pressure is pressure measured above atmospheric; absolute is total including atmospheric.
Gauge pressure includes atmospheric; absolute does not.
They are the same in liquids.
Absolute pressure is always smaller than gauge pressure.

sets.q: A diver's depth gauge reads 50 000 Pa. What does this reading represent?
sets.given: Depth gauges measure pressure above atmospheric (gauge pressure).
opts: Gauge pressure — the pressure above atmospheric
Absolute pressure — the total pressure on the diver

sets.q: At the water surface (h = 0), what is the gauge pressure?
A tire pressure gauge reads 200 000 Pa. The actual absolute pressure in the tire is:
Which pressure is always greater: gauge pressure or absolute pressure?
At what depth in fresh water is the hydrostatic pressure equal to 70 000 Pa? (ρ = 1 000 kg/m³, g = 10 m/s²)
At what depth does water exert a gauge pressure of 25 000 Pa? (ρ = 1 000 kg/m³, g = 10 m/s²)
How deep must one dive for the gauge pressure to reach 150 000 Pa? (ρ = 1 000 kg/m³, g = 10 m/s²)
At what depth is the hydrostatic pressure 40 000 Pa? (ρ = 1 000 kg/m³, g = 10 m/s²)
What depth gives a hydrostatic pressure of 200 000 Pa in fresh water? (ρ = 1 000 kg/m³, g = 10 m/s²)
Calculate the hydrostatic pressure at 5 m depth in seawater. (ρ = 1 025 kg/m³, g = 10 m/s²)
Find the gauge pressure at 10 m depth in seawater. (ρ = 1 025 kg/m³, g = 10 m/s²)
At 3 m depth in seawater (ρ = 1 025 kg/m³, g = 10 m/s²), what is the hydrostatic pressure?
Why is hydrostatic pressure greater in seawater than fresh water at the same depth?
sets.given: P = ρgh. Seawater: ρ ≈ 1 025 kg/m³. Fresh water: ρ = 1 000 kg/m³.
opts: Seawater has higher density, so ρgh is larger
Depth affects only fresh water pressure

sets.q: Hydrostatic pressure at 8 m in seawater (ρ = 1 025 kg/m³, g = 10 m/s²)?
Hydrostatic pressure depends on which factors?
sets.given: Formula: P = ρgh — identify the variables.
opts: Fluid density, gravitational acceleration, and depth

sets.q: Two identical containers are filled with water to different heights. Where is the pressure at the base greater?
sets.given: P = ρgh. Same ρ and g; compare h.
opts: Taller container — greater h gives greater pressure
Shorter container — less weight of water
They are equal regardless of height

sets.q: Two pools: one deep and narrow, one shallow and wide, both filled with the same liquid. At equal depth, how does pressure compare?
sets.given: P = ρgh. Pressure depends only on depth, density, and g — not container shape.
opts: Equal — pressure at the same depth is the same
Higher in the narrow pool — more weight per area
Higher in the wide pool — more total water

sets.q: The 'hydrostatic paradox' states that pressure at a given depth in a liquid depends only on:
sets.given: P = ρgh — examine which variables appear.
opts: Depth (h), fluid density (ρ), and gravitational acceleration (g)
The weight of fluid above the point
The surface area of the container

sets.q: Which change does NOT affect the hydrostatic pressure at a given point?
sets.given: P = ρgh. Identify which factor is not included.
opts: Increasing the horizontal width of the container

sets.q: A dam holds back water to a depth of 40 m. What is the hydrostatic pressure at the base of the dam? (ρ = 1 000 kg/m³, g = 10 m/s²)
A submarine operates at 200 m depth in seawater (ρ = 1 025 kg/m³, g = 10 m/s²). What is the gauge pressure?
A diver at 15 m depth in fresh water (ρ = 1 000 kg/m³, g = 10 m/s²). What gauge pressure do they experience?
The Hinatuan Enchanted River is about 8 m deep. Estimate the hydrostatic pressure at its deepest point. (ρ = 1 025 kg/m³, g = 10 m/s²)
sets.given: P = ρgh (use seawater density — it's a saltwater river)

sets.q: A scuba diver is at 25 m depth in fresh water (ρ = 1 000 kg/m³, g = 10 m/s²). What is the absolute pressure? (Patm = 101 325 Pa)
The hydrostatic pressure at a certain depth is 60 000 Pa in fresh water. What is the density of a liquid that produces 72 000 Pa at the same depth? (g = 10 m/s²)
sets.given: P = ρgh. Same h and g. New P / Old P = new ρ / old ρ.

sets.q: Mercury (ρ = 13 600 kg/m³) fills a tube to height 0.76 m. What pressure does it exert? (g = 10 m/s²)
Oil (ρ = 800 kg/m³) fills a tank to 5 m depth. What is the hydrostatic pressure at the base? (g = 10 m/s²)
Saltwater (ρ = 1 025 kg/m³) and fresh water (ρ = 1 000 kg/m³) both have 6 m depth. Which has greater base pressure?
sets.given: P = ρgh. Compare for same h and g.

sets.q: Glycerin (ρ = 1 260 kg/m³) fills a vessel to 2 m depth. What is the pressure at the bottom? (g = 10 m/s²)
A U-tube manometer has fresh water (ρ = 1 000 kg/m³) in one arm 0.3 m above the other arm. What is the pressure difference between the two arms? (g = 10 m/s²)
One arm of a U-tube is 0.5 m higher than the other. Filled with water (ρ = 1 000 kg/m³). What pressure difference does this represent? (g = 10 m/s²)
A water column 0.8 m high is used to measure gauge pressure. What pressure does it represent? (ρ = 1 000 kg/m³, g = 10 m/s²)
A mercury manometer shows a height difference of 0.15 m. What pressure difference does it indicate? (ρHg = 13 600 kg/m³, g = 10 m/s²)
Oil (ρ = 800 kg/m³) in a U-tube shows a height difference of 0.4 m. What is the gauge pressure? (g = 10 m/s²)
Why do divers experience ear pain when swimming deeper?
sets.given: Consider how hydrostatic pressure changes with depth.
opts: Increased water pressure with depth compresses air pockets in the ear
The water becomes colder at depth
Sound waves travel differently at depth
The weight of the water above increases the swimmer's mass

sets.q: Why are deep-sea submarines built with thick, curved hulls?
sets.given: At great depth, P = ρgh becomes very large.
opts: To withstand the enormous hydrostatic pressure at depth
To reduce drag in fast currents

sets.q: Why are water tanks and reservoirs placed at high elevation above the area they serve?
sets.given: P = ρgh. Higher h means higher pressure.
opts: Greater height creates greater water pressure for delivery

sets.q: A dam wall is made thicker at the bottom than at the top. Why?
sets.given: P = ρgh increases with depth h.
opts: Greater hydrostatic pressure at greater depth requires more structural support
The bottom needs more weight to stay stable
The top must be light to reduce cost
Water pressure is the same throughout

sets.q: A sealed bag of chips appears puffed up when brought to a high mountain top. This is because:
opts: External atmospheric pressure decreases, so the bag expands outward
The chips absorb air at high altitude
The bag's volume decreases at altitude
